behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface (parameter names, types, return type)
behaviors.justification: Must wire real Python parsing/AST validation logic with mocked LLM output to catch failures in signature extraction and type annotation handling. Pure mocks can't detect parsing bugs; real execution/import isn't needed if using ast.parse() and inspect-like logic.
behaviors.test_description: Mock LLM to return syntactically valid Python code with various function definitions. Call code generation service. Parse generated code using ast.parse() or equivalent. Extract function signature and verify: parameter names match exactly, type annotations are present and correct, return type matches specification. Test across scenarios: positional-only, keyword-only, defaults, Optional types, custom classes. Verify it catches mismatches like wrong parameter order, missing/incorrect type hints, renamed parameters.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided test cases on first or subsequent attempt
behaviors.justification: The phrase 'subsequent attempt' indicates refinement is part of success criteria. Real failure would be: code fails, refinement doesn't help, or full loop is broken. This requires real LLM calls (non-deterministic code generation), real test subprocess execution, and real refinement loop working together—cannot be fully tested with mocks because refinement effectiveness depends on real LLM behavior reacting to real test failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call code generation service with function specification and test suite. Use real LLM (not mocked). Execute generated code in subprocess against real tests to get actual pass/fail. Allow full generate→test→refine cycle up to 3 attempts. Verify either first attempt passes all tests OR refinement loop produces code that passes. Test with both simple functions (pass first attempt) and complex functions requiring refinement. Assert generated code actually executes without runtime errors and produces correct outputs, not just test assertion passes.

behaviors.description: Refinement includes failure messages in next LLM prompt
behaviors.justification: Testing prompt construction and message inclusion logic. Real failure: refinement prompt omits test failures or truncates them. Can catch by mocking LLM and inspecting input to verify failures are included; doesn't need real LLM or real test execution because behavior is deterministic prompt-building, not dependent on LLM quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure scenario where generated code will fail tests. Mock LLM to return failing code on first call. On second LLM call (refinement), intercept/capture the prompt being sent. Verify it includes: test failure messages verbatim, assertion errors, exception output, and error traces from first attempt. Test with multiple failing test cases to ensure ALL failures are included, not just first one. Verify failures are readable (not truncated, properly escaped, appropriately formatted).

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error with all attempts
behaviors.justification: Testing loop termination logic and error response structure—pure business logic that's deterministic. Real failure: loop doesn't stop (attempts 4+) or error response is malformed. Mocks are sufficient: mock LLM to always fail, mock test execution to always fail, verify termination and response format. Doesn't require real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call service with function specification. Mock LLM to always return code that fails all tests. Mock test execution to always report failures. Verify service makes exactly 3 attempts (not 2, not 4). Verify after 3 attempts, service returns structured error response containing: all three generated implementations, test results for each, and metadata indicating max attempts reached. Validate error response matches spec (correct JSON schema, required fields present). Verify no fourth attempt occurs even if service is called again.

behaviors.description: Full loop produces working implementation for function requiring refinement
behaviors.justification: Capstone test of entire system. Phrase 'typically gets wrong' indicates function needs real LLM quality to eventually solve. Real failures: LLM generates bad code, test execution is broken, refinement doesn't improve code, or loop doesn't integrate properly. Requires: real LLM calls (non-deterministic), real subprocess test execution, real refinement loop reacting to real failures. Mocks hide whether refinement actually works because mock LLM won't realistically improve code based on failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call code generation service with function specification known to be difficult (impossible or very likely to fail on single LLM call, but solvable with refinement). Use real LLM—do not mock. Execute generated code in real subprocess against real test suite. Allow full generate→test→refine cycle with real LLM calls on refinement attempts. Verify final implementation passes all tests. Verify refinement actually improved code (compare quality/correctness of attempt 3 vs attempt 1). Validate that later LLM calls are actually using test failure information to guide improvements, not just making random changes.
